req.title: Delete patient (soft)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates a soft-delete operation on a FHIR Patient resource by a system-admin. Three orthogonal test scenarios are needed: (1) EP positive – admin successfully soft-deletes an active patient, verifying the 200/204 response and that the resource is marked deleted while history is preserved; (2) NEG negative – a non-admin caller receives HTTP 403 and no deletion occurs; (3) STC boundary – deleting an already-deleted patient (re-delete idempotency / state transition from 'deleted' to 'deleted') verifying the server handles the repeated delete gracefully and history still accumulates without data loss. These cover EP (valid admin partition), NEG (invalid role partition), and STC (state: active→deleted→deleted transition).

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient_success
preconditions: Admin credentials are available via <admin_token fixture>
A patient resource exists and is active (created via <create_patient helper>)
actions: Create a transient Patient resource to obtain a valid {id}
Send GET /Patient/{id} to verify soft-delete marker
Send GET /Patient/{id}/_history to verify history is preserved
expected: GET /Patient/{id} returns HTTP 410 Gone with 'inactive' or deleted marker in body
GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns HTTP 200 with at least one history entry
trace: R-HLT-004: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient_success(admin_token, base_url, create_patient):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_token, base_url)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}

    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Expected 200/204, got {del_resp.status_code}"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410 Gone, got {get_resp.status_code}"

    hist_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=headers)
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 for history, got {hist_resp.status_code}"
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "History must contain at least one entry after soft-delete"

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_patient_forbidden
preconditions: A non-admin (role: practitioner) token is available via <practitioner_token fixture>
Admin token is available via <admin_token fixture>
A patient resource exists (created via <create_patient helper>)
actions: Create a transient Patient resource as admin to obtain a valid {id}
Confirm the patient still exists by fetching it with admin token
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code indicating forbidden
Subsequent GET /Patient/{id} by admin returns 200 (resource not deleted)
trace: R-HLT-004: only system-admin actor is authorized
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_admin_delete_patient_forbidden(admin_token, practitioner_token, base_url, create_patient):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_token, base_url)
    non_admin_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {practitioner_token}"}
    admin_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=non_admin_headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403 Forbidden, got {del_resp.status_code}"

    body = del_resp.json()
    resource_type = body.get("resourceType", "")
    assert resource_type == "OperationOutcome", "Body must be an OperationOutcome on 403"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Patient must still exist after forbidden delete attempt"

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_already_deleted_patient_idempotent
preconditions: A patient resource exists and has already been soft-deleted once (state: deleted)
actions: Issue first DELETE /Patient/{id} to transition state from active to deleted
Issue second DELETE /Patient/{id} to attempt delete from already-deleted state
expected: Second DELETE returns 200, 204, or 404 (server-defined idempotency; must NOT return 500)
GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns 200 with history entries >= 1 and no data loss
trace: R-HLT-004: deletion shall not remove history (re-delete boundary)
R-HLT-004: state transition active->deleted->deleted must not corrupt data
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_soft_delete_already_deleted_patient_idempotent(admin_token, base_url, create_patient):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_token, base_url)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    first_del = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert first_del.status_code in (200, 204), f"First delete failed: {first_del.status_code}"

    second_del = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=headers)
    assert second_del.status_code in (200, 204, 404), (
        f"Second delete must not be a server error, got {second_del.status_code}"
    )
    assert second_del.status_code != 500, "Server error on re-delete is unacceptable"

    hist_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=headers)
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200, f"History endpoint failed: {hist_resp.status_code}"
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "History must be preserved after repeated soft-delete"
